name: Igala
notes: Igala, a Volta-Niger (Yoruboid) language of Kogi State, Nigeria. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Written in a Latin orthography with the letters ẹ, ọ and tone marks; Wikidata records no writing system for the item and no published letter-to-phoneme table was consulted, so no grapheme map is claimed. Igala is tonal (three level tones), and tone marking would have to be handled. wikidata_qid left unset: the code's Wikidata row resolves to the Wikimedia project item, not the language item; not recorded rather than record a wrong id.
